compiled_state.title: Integrate ACCESSIBILITY_REVIEWER agent into DSO review pipeline
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Integrate the mgifford/ACCESSIBILITY.md Accessibility Reviewer agent (https://github.com/mgifford/ACCESSIBILITY.md/blob/main/agents/ACCESSIBILITY_REVIEWER.md) into DSO's code review pipeline as a dedicated accessibility overlay.

## What the Agent Does

An IAAP CPWA-certified accessibility auditor agent that uses a four-phase adversarial Red Team / Blue Team pipeline:

- **Phase 1 (Red Team — Functional Friction):** Simulates 6 disability personas on Critical User Journeys:
  1. Screen Reader Navigator (Non-Visual)
  2. Power Keyboard User (Motor Limit)
  3. Magnification Expert (Low Vision, 400% zoom)
  4. Cognitive Strategist (Neurodivergent)
  5. Vestibular User (Motion Sensitivity)
  6. Distracted/Fatigued User (Situational Limit)

- **Phase 2 (Red Team — Semantic Audit):** Code-aware manual audit targeting the ~60-70% of WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA that requires human reasoning (1.3.2, 1.1.1, 2.4.4, 3.3.3, 3.3.4, 3.3.7, 3.3.8, 2.5.7, 4.1.3).

- **Phase 3 (Deterministic Integration):** Consumes Axe Core automated tool output as ground truth for mechanical compliance.

- **Phase 4 (Blue Team — Synthesis):** Merges findings, scrubs false positives, resolves conflicts via web research, and escalates severity when issues cluster on critical user journeys.

## Integration Points

1. Add as a review overlay in the code review workflow — activated when the complexity classifier detects UI/frontend changes (similar to security and performance overlays)
2. Output format: structured Markdown with Finding Title, Severity, Persona Impact, Evidence (DOM snippet), WCAG Mapping, and Technical Remediation
3. Integrate with Axe Core output in CI for Phase 3 deterministic input
4. Add to preplanning as an accessibility reviewer perspective using the 6 persona framework
5. Create a standalone /dso:accessibility-check skill for on-demand audits

## Key Design Principles to Preserve

- Prioritizes functional task success over checkbox compliance
- Cumulative severity escalation: multiple Low issues on the same critical path = Critical barrier
- Strict hallucination guardrail: no invented DOM elements
- Actionable remediation only: must cite specific ARIA attributes, HTML changes, or CSS adjustments
- Division of labor: automated tools handle mechanical checks, LLM handles contextual reasoning

Source: https://github.com/mgifford/ACCESSIBILITY.md
